From the raw engine run metrics I recomputed Wilson one-sided lower bounds for 600/600 covered exact and 300/300 strict control refusals as 0.9955102074846524 and 0.9910605512394408, with six store violations, matching the verdict and analysis output. The abstain-all baseline recomputes to covered exact rate 0.0 and control refused-any rate 1.0, while answer-all recomputes to covered exact rate 1.0 and control refused-any rate 0.0, with the expected abstain-all strict-code count of 0. I read the engine and instrument paths and confirmed the engine builds deterministic sorted indexes over pinned axiom and world records, uses no randomness or heuristic scoring, and does not read eval expected fields; expected values are consumed only in the instrument scorer. Refusals are fail-closed ERR_* codes, and engine answers carry provenance and license data, with the scorer checking provenance ids against the world store. Applying the frozen verdict rules top-down gives rule 0 false, rule 1 false, rule 2 PASS true, and because no confirmed non-runner audit record exists and the runner was runner-2 rather than auditor-2, PASS-PENDING-AUDIT is the correct G-6 state."}
